question.content: For the two-mass system rotating about point A, find the angular-momentum vector at the shown instant in the i1-j1-k1 basis. Answer format: a single vector expression. The variable names may only be Omega, phi, i1, j1, k1, m, and L; use ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Build the 3D rotating geometry at A with x y z and coincident x1 y1 z1 bases.
step_1.key_point: At this instant, rotating and inertial bases are aligned.

step_3.content: Use omega along z1 and compute r x (omega x r) contributions for m1 and m2.
step_3.key_point: Angular momentum has coupled i1 and k1 components when phi is nonzero.

step_5.content: Differentiate H in the inertial frame and map components onto i1 j1 k1.
step_5.key_point: The j1 term scales with Omega^2 and i1 k1 terms scale with Omega_dot.

step_7.content: For torque only along z1, add a symmetric counter-mass mirrored in z1 to cancel cross coupling.
step_7.key_point: Make x1 y1 and x1 z1 symmetry planes so the chosen basis becomes principal.
